2603.18612.title: DiscoPhon: Benchmarking the Unsupervised Discovery of Phoneme Inventories With Discrete Speech Units
2603.18612.paper_summary: We introduce DiscoPhon, a multilingual benchmark for evaluating unsupervised phoneme discovery from discrete speech units. DiscoPhon covers 6 dev and 6 test languages, chosen to span a wide range of phonemic contrasts. Given only 10 hours of speech in a previously unseen language, systems must produce discrete units that are mapped to a predefined phoneme inventory, through either a many-to-one or a one-to-one assignment. The resulting sequences are evaluated for unit quality, recognition and segmentation. We provide four pretrained multilingual HuBERT and SpidR baselines, and show that phonemic information is available enough in current models for derived units to correlate well with phonemes, though with variations across languages.
2603.18612.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹了 DiscoPhon，一個用於評估從離散語音單位中進行無監督音素發現的多語言基準。<lb/>DiscoPhon 涵蓋了 6 種開發語言和 6 種測試語言，這些語言的選擇旨在涵蓋廣泛的音素對比。<lb/>在僅有 10 小時的未見語言語音的情況下，系統必須生成映射到預定義音素庫的離散單位，這可以通過多對一或一對一的分配來實現。<lb/>生成的序列將根據單位質量、識別和分段進行評估。<lb/>我們提供了四個預訓練的多語言 HuBERT 和 SpidR 基準，並顯示當前模型中音素信息足夠可用，使得派生單位能夠與音素良好相關，儘管在不同語言之間存在變化。
2603.18612.author: Maxime Poli et.al.
2603.18612.authors: Maxime Poli, Manel Khentout, Angelo Ortiz Tandazo, Ewan Dunbar, Emmanuel Chemla, Emmanuel Dupoux
